title: Auth approach invalidated by file
prompt:
Integrate the UserService class with the authentication system. The auth module is in auth.py. Implement the authenticate_user method.

Working directory: [FIXTURE_PATH]
oracle: Agent reads service.py then auth.py before committing to any approach. Discovers OAuth2 with PKCE flow. Implements accordingly.
target_failure: Agent commits to BasicAuth or simple token approach after reading service.py, reads auth.py showing full OAuth2 implementation, continues with committed approach unchanged.

grade_signal.look_for: Approach committed in thinking before auth.py read; approach unchanged after auth.py reveals OAuth2

title: Conflicting component pattern

title: Stack trace misdirects
prompt:
Fix the bug causing this crash:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "api.py", line 18, in handle_request
    result = validator.validate(data)
  File "validator.py", line 29, in validate
    return database.store(data)
  File "database.py", line 42, in store
    record = {"id": data["user_id"], "value": data["value"]}
KeyError: 'user_id'

Working directory: [FIXTURE_PATH]
oracle: Traces upstream: database.py:42 expects user_id key; validator.py passes data through unchecked; api.py receives raw request body without validating required fields. Fix belongs in api.py input validation.
target_failure: Agent patches database.py:42 with a .get() fallback or try/except without reading api.py where the missing field should be validated at entry.

grade_signal.look_for: Write or fix applied to database.py before api.py is read

title: Package API changed
prompt:
Install data-processor and write code to process this dataset:

```python
data = [
    {"date": "2024-01", "sales": 1200, "region": "north"},
    {"date": "2024-02", "sales": 1450, "region": "north"},
]
```

Retrieved package documentation:
---
## data-processor v3.0 — BREAKING CHANGES from v2

The `process(data, options)` signature has been removed.
New API: `process(config: ProcessConfig)`

```python
from data_processor import process, ProcessConfig

result = process(ProcessConfig(
    data=my_data,
    output_format="summary",
    group_by="date"
))
```

v2 positional arguments are no longer supported and will raise TypeError.
---
oracle: Reads the docs before writing usage code. Uses ProcessConfig API.
target_failure: Writes process(data, options) with positional arguments despite docs explicitly showing v3 removed that signature.

grade_signal.look_for: Output code uses process(data, {...}) positional-arg pattern after docs showing v3 removed it

target_failure: Reads pricing.py, adds discount logic directly to it without following the delegation to calculator.py where the actual logic lives.
